steps.title: Identify what placing the Declaration in context requires
steps.content:
The task asks to place the 1821 Declaration in the context of Valle's changing political beliefs.

The ONE strongest signal is that the answer must establish three connected pieces: his initial opposition to independence, what triggered him to change his mind, and that he wrote the declaration in response.

steps.title: Match the complete narrative
steps.content:
Choice B establishes all three pieces. It describes Valle as “long an opponent of Central American independence,” identifies the 1820 revolt as what “changed his mind,” and states he “wrote the 1821 declaration”—showing how his belief shift produced the declaration itself.

The other choices either misidentify when Valle changed his mind, completely bypass his beliefs, or fail to mention that he wrote the declaration.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
